proof: Choose supported version and document Ruby, Node, database, and upgrade path before build.
Use Decidim app generation flow rather than cloning core source for a normal installation.
Create database role and separate development, staging, and production databases.
Confirm frontend assets build and precompile in the chosen environment.
Confirm ImageMagick/LibVips and ActiveStorage decisions before attachments.
Do not send real notifications until sender identity, notice, and unsubscribe are approved.
Configure mail digest, reminders, open-data export, inactive participant cleanup, and other required tasks.
Create system admin, create organisation, then invite organisation admin through controlled route.
Test least privilege, admin revocation, participant manager route, and audit evidence privately.
Run backup and restore evidence privately before real pilot data exists.
Keep public fake demo, private staging, and production data isolated.
Name uptime, errors, dependency updates, security patches, and incident contact.
Disable precise location features unless privacy and data reviewers approve.
Hold open data exports until redaction and IDS/privacy review pass.
